front: Novel: Juan Preciado visits the ghost-inhabited town of Comala to find his father; all residents — Eduviges Dyada, Damiana Cisneros, Abundio Martinez — turn out to be dead
Novel: the title character loves Susana San Juan and lets the town die of hunger out of spite while mourning her death
Novel: Garcia Marquez allegedly read this novel twice in one night and could recite it forwards and backwards; said he felt blocked as a novelist until reading its author
Short Story: 35 years after killing Don Lupe Terreros over cattle pasturing, a man faces a firing squad and makes the title plea to his son Justino
back: "Tell Them Not to Kill Me!" / The Burning Plain (Juan Rulfo)
work: The Burning Plain (El Llano en llamas)

front: Short Story: the narrator and his sick brother's wife Natalia arrange the brother's death during a pilgrimage to a shrine
work: The Burning Plain (El Llano en llamas)

front: Short Story: the narrator worries that Tacha will end up like her disreputable sisters after a cow is washed away by a flood
back: "Because We Are So Poor" / The Burning Plain (Juan Rulfo)
work: The Burning Plain (El Llano en llamas)

front: Short Story: a father carries his wounded bandit son Ignacio to see a doctor
back: "No Oyes Ladrar los Perros" / The Burning Plain (Juan Rulfo)
work: The Burning Plain (El Llano en llamas)

front: Short Story: the title story features a mock bullfighting execution; opens with the epigraph 'Now they killed [a dog] / But [her offspring] remain'
work: The Burning Plain (El Llano en llamas)
